pubmed_links: [Das Chakraborty et al. (2012) Dysregulation of DGCR6 and DGCR6L: psychopathological outcomes in 22q11.2 deletion syndrome](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22832905/); [Liu et al. (2002) Genetic variation at the 22q11 PRODH2/DGCR6 locus increases susceptibility to schizophrenia](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/11891283/); [Edelmann et al. (2001) Two functional copies of the DGCR6 gene on chromosome 22q11 due to ancestral locus duplication](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/11157784/)
summary: DGCR6L is a paralog of DGCR6 located within the 22q11.2 deletion region, and epigenetic dysregulation of both genes has been associated with childhood psychopathological outcomes—including anxiety disorders—in 22q11.2 deletion syndrome, a condition conferring high risk for schizophrenia and other psychiatric disorders. Variants at the broader PRODH2/DGCR6 locus have also been independently linked to increased schizophrenia susceptibility. (emerging evidence)
